title: Will future CEOs be at a disadvantage without engineering? — mentor-Q seed (2021)
html:
Will future CEOs be at a disadvantage without engineering? — mentor-Q seed (2021)

Source: 2021-11-05 Apple Note. Two parts: a mentor-conversation question on engineering-as-CEO-prereq, and a self-honest paragraph on drugs/drinking creep.

TL;DR

  • "Do you think CEOs of the future will be at a disadvantage if they are not engineers or don't have an understanding of technology?" — explains why Yahir later pivots from OSU business → CS major. The thesis is captured here as a pre-pivot question, not yet an answer.
  • "What is one thing you recommend we do in college?" — shows Yahir was actively interviewing mentors for college-strategy meta-advice; same posture as the 2023 AGM startup advice note.
  • "You don't know what you have become until you have become it" — addiction-creep observation: drugs, "doesn't happen, then it starts and u forgot how it started."
  • "Yeah you should probably stop drinking and smoking. You kind of new past then" — explicit self-warning to himself, OSU sophomore year. Predates the 2021 12 05 drugs as Mario banana note by 1 month.
  • OSU sophomore year mental snapshot: simultaneously upskilling toward technical-CEO archetype + watching his own substance-creep with concern.

Connections

  • osu
  • 2023 07 05 startup advice
  • 2021 12 05 cameron crypto and drugs thesis
  • career archetypes spy leader creator
